revision.text:
[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site |name=Deyama Horizontal Tomb Cluster |alternate_name= |image=[[File:出山横穴墓群 2009.03.21 - panoramio (1).jpg|200px]] |caption=Facility for the 8th Tomb (March 21, 2009) |location={{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, [[:en:Mitaka City|Mitaka City]], Osawa |coordinates={{coordinates degrees minutes seconds|35|40|41.9|N|139|31|53.6|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark_map_size:20000|display=inline,title|qq=}}; {{Coord|35.67830555555555|N|139.53155555555554|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|built=7th century |alternate_name=Designated as a city cultural property on March 22, 1994{{Cite web|language=Japanese|author=Sports and Culture Department, Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.mitaka.lg.jp/c_service/031/031091.html|title=List of Historic Site in the City|work=Mitaka City|date=2018-12-27|accessdate=2022-03-30|qq=}} |map_size=10 horizontal tombs |map={{location map|Japan Western Tokyo|map_dot_label=Deyama
Horizontal Tomb Cluster|coordinates=35|coordinates=40|coordinates=41.9|lon_deg=139|lon_min=31|lon_sec=53.6|mark=横穴墓.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Tokyo|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|40|41.9|N|139|31|53.6|E|qq=}}|qq=}} }} '''Deyama Horizontal Tomb Cluster''' (出山横穴墓群) is a cluster of horizontal tombs located in Ōsawa, [[:en:Mitaka City|Mitaka City]], {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, Japan. It consists of 10 tombs, with the 8th tomb Historic Site1 as a [[Tokyo Prefectural Historic Site]] in 1994.{{Cite web|author=Department of Sports and Culture, Lifelong Learning Division|url=https://www.city.mitaka.lg.jp/c_service/031/031091.html|title=List of Historic Site in the City|publisher=Mitaka City|date=2018-12-27|accessdate=2022-03-30|qq=}} == Discovery and Excavation == The Deyama Horizontal Tomb Cluster is part of a series of burial sites found along the {{ill|Nogawa River|en|Nogawa River|qid=Q11645427|lt=Nogawa River|1=Nogawa River}}'s northern banks within the [[Kokubunji Escarpment]]. These tombs, dated to the mid-7th to early 8th century, are among seven distinct horizontal tomb clusters in the region. The [[Tenmondai Kounai Kofun|Tenmondai Kōnai Kofun]], located nearby, is believed to house the chief of this society, based on its prominent location and construction period. The first record of the tombs dates back to 1884, mentioned in the magazine Musashino Sōshi. Subsequent rediscoveries and excavations occurred over decades: - In 1963, the cluster was officially recorded as Mitaka City's first recognized tomb cluster. - Excavations began in 1978, uncovering remnants of Tomb 1. - In 1984, construction projects led to the discovery and partial excavation of additional tombs. - In 1993, Tomb 8 was selected for detailed excavation and public presentation. By 1994, Tomb 8 was Historic Site1 a Tokyo Prefectural Historic Site, with further radar surveys uncovering Tombs 9 and 10. == Geographical and Historical Context == The tombs are situated on the Kokubunji Escarpment, a river terrace typeed by the {{ill|Tama River|en|Tama River|qid=Q1063006|lt=Tama River|1=Tama River}} and characterized by {{ill|Loam|en|Loam|qid=Q1131303|lt=Kanto Loam|1=Loam}} layers. The horizontal tombs align with the Tokyo Pumice Layer, a geological omitted minimizing groundwater intrusion. This careful placement reflects the burial practices of the period. The area is rich in archaeological significance, with burial goods spanning the {{ill|Paleolithic|en|Paleolithic|qid=Q40203|lt=Paleolithic|1=Paleolithic}} to [[:en:Jōmon|Jōmon]] periods. Nearby, remnants of a Jōmon dwelling and associated tools were found, indicating the area's long-term habitation. == Structure of Tomb 8 == Tomb 8 is notable for its size and well-preserved construction: * '''area''': The burial chamber measures 3.3m in length, with a maximum width of 2.4m and a height of 1.8m. * '''Entrance''': The entrance passage and forecourt feature stone paving, with the chamber divided into front and rear sections by boundary stones. * '''Materials''': Large {{ill|Andesite|en|Andesite|qid=Q181908|lt=Andesite|1=Andesite}} rocks from the udder {{ill|Tama River|en|Tama River|qid=Q1063006|lt=Tama River|1=Tama River}} were used for structural elements, including the entrance ceiling. * '''Tools''': Tool marks suggest the use of traditional agricultural tools for excavation. burial goods include fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, dated to the mid-7th century, and skeletal remains of four individuals, analyzed for dietary and familial connections. == Conservation and Public Access == Following excavation, Tomb 8 was preserved with environmental controls and opened to the public in 1996. Visitors can view the interior through glass panels, with educational displays explaining the tomb's construction and findings. == Related Sites == The cluster is part of a broader network of horizontal tombs along the Nogawa River, with over 60 identified tombs. The nearby Tenmondai Kofun offers further insights into the social hierarchy of the period. == Significance == Deyama Horizontal Tomb Cluster is a key site for understanding 7th-century burial practices and social structures in the Tama region. Its Historic Site as a historic site underscores its cultural and archaeological importance. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.mitaka-iseki.jp/kikaku4/yokoana1.htm Special Feature on Mitaka Tombs] * [https://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/bunkazai/index.html Tokyo designation1 Database] {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|出山横穴墓群|version=97905338|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Deyama Graves}} [[ja:出山横穴墓群]] {{wikidata link|Q11395609}} [[Category:Archaeological sites in Tokyo]] [[Category:Archaeological sites on the Kokubunji Cliff]] [[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Mitaka, Tokyo]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
